saveframes.name: sample_1
tags:
microcrystalline GB3: 
GB3 (~30 mg/mL) in 50 mM sodium phosphate, pH 6.5, 0.02% (w/v) sodium azide
buffer crystallized using 2-methylpentane-2,4-diol, isopropanol, deionized water
in a 2:1:1 (v/v) ratio

data: Delaglio, Grzesiek, Vuister, Zhu, Pfeifer and Bax

saveframes.name: chemical_shift_reference_1
tags:
All shifts are referenced  to the CH peak of solid adamantane at 40.48ppm
according to the  recommendations of Morcombe and Zilm (J. Magn. Reson. 2003,
162, 479-486).

data: separate tube (no insert) similar to the experimental sample tube

saveframes.name: assigned_chem_shift_list_1
tags:
For most resonances the errors in the chemical shifts were derived based upon
variations observed in multiple data sets. For those resonances, where the
chemical shift was obtained from a single data set, the error was obtained as
the peak width at half height divided by the signal to noise ratio.

The "1 MET N" is an N-terminal (i.e., NH3+) group, and the "LYZ NZ" corresponds 
to NH3+ group too.
